title: tender malaysian cabbage bao
description: floral and tender malaysian dish featuring red cabbage, macrobiotic-friendly ingredients. perfect for a brunch setting.
instructions: in a small bowl, combine yeast and coconut sugar. let sit for 5 minutes until frothy.
in a large mixing bowl, combine flour and pink salt.
add yeast mixture, sesame oil, and water to the dry ingredients. mix until a dough forms.
knead the dough for 10 minutes until smooth.
place dough in a greased bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
preheat a steamer basket with boiling water.
roll out the dough to 1/4 inch thickness. use a round cookie cutter to cut out circles of dough.
place a tablespoon of red cabbage in the center of each dough circle. fold the dough over the cabbage to form a half-moon shape. press edges to seal.
place the buns in the steamer basket, seam-side down. steam for 10 minutes until cooked through.
brush the buns with apple cider vinegar and top with sesame seeds.
serve warm or at room temperature.
